title: phlomoides betonicoides
intro: Phlomoides betonicoides, also known as Mongolian mint or Mongolian plantain, is a plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to Central Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving headaches, and improving respiratory problems. Phlomoides betonicoides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be dried and used as a herbal supplement or added to traditional remedies like soups and infusions.
